# pixi-reels

pixi-reels 1.0.0 is a reel engine for PixiJS v8.
It ships reel-only primitives. Win math, paytable math, RNG, and audio live in consumer code.
This file inlines every guide and recipe for offline LLM context.

Site: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev
Repo: https://github.com/schmooky/pixi-reels
Package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/pixi-reels

Generated: 2026-06-20T11:23:30.013Z

## Quick start

```bash
npm install pixi-reels pixi.js gsap
```

```ts
import { Application } from 'pixi.js';
import { ReelSetBuilder, SpriteSymbol } from 'pixi-reels';

const app = new Application();
await app.init({ width: 800, height: 480 });

const reelSet = new ReelSetBuilder()
  .reels(5).visibleRows(3).symbolSize(120, 120)
  .symbols(r => r.register('cherry', SpriteSymbol, { textures: { cherry: cherryTex } }))
  .ticker(app.ticker)
  .build();
app.stage.addChild(reelSet);

const result = reelSet.spin();
reelSet.setResult([{ visible: ['cherry','cherry','cherry'] }, /* one per reel */ ]);
await result;
```

## Guides

### Big symbols
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/big-symbols/
A big symbol is a single symbol that visually occupies an N×M block of cells. Declared via SymbolData.size; server places the anchor cell only and the engine paints OCCUPIED across the rest. Includes block lookup, validation, and mask interaction.

### Buffer indexing
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/buffer-indexing/
How ColumnTarget indices map to cells in initialFrame and setResult. Visible rows, buffer-above, buffer-below.

### Cascades. the mental model
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/cascades/
Two moments, three phases, gravity-correct refills. The mental model behind .tumble() so you stop fighting the API.

### Cheats & testing
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/cheats-and-testing/
Force deterministic outcomes, run spin lifecycles in Node, write mechanic tests that never flake.

### Debugging
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/debugging/
Snapshot the reel state as JSON, render an ASCII grid, trace every event.

### Getting started
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/getting-started/
Install pixi-reels and run your first reel set in under 2 minutes.

### Hold & Win
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/hold-and-win/
The Hold & Win lifecycle end to end — the mental model, the state machine, the events, and how to wire the board to your game client.

### MultiWays
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/multiways/
Per-spin row variation. Each reel can land on a different row count between minRows and maxRows, with cell height derived from a fixed reel pixel height. The engine reshapes each reel between SPIN and STOP and AdjustPhase tweens any pin overlays; pins migrate via originRow.

### Nudge
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/nudge/
Push a reel down or up by one or more positions after it has stopped, revealing caller-supplied symbols.

### Per-reel geometry
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/per-reel-geometry/
Each reel can have its own row count, cell height, and offset. Covers static pyramid layouts, the mask strategy that clips them cleanly, and how this interacts with MultiWays and big symbols (which have their own guides).

### Pins
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/pins/
CellPin is the cell-persistence primitive. It survives spins, migrates on reshape, and works with any ReelSymbol subclass. This guide covers lifecycle, events, movePin, and Spine-specific animation hooks.

### Recipe previews
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/recipe-previews/
How to give a recipe a card thumbnail and inline body images. Convention-first — drop a file in public/ and it appears. Missing assets fall back to a generated placeholder, so the page always looks finished.

### Speed modes
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/speed-modes/
Normal, turbo, super-turbo. and adding your own.

### Spin lifecycle
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/spin-lifecycle/
The full lifecycle of a spin. phases, events, and how to hook in.

### Symbols
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/symbols/
Register and implement reel symbols. sprites, animations, Spine, or custom.

### Win animations
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/win-animations/
Highlight winning symbols, cycle through lines, and dim the losers.

### Build your first cascade slot
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/your-first-cascade/
From `npm install` to a working cascade slot in ~80 lines of TypeScript. No prior pixi-reels knowledge required.

### Your first reelset
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/guides/your-first-reelset/
Build, spin, and react to a reel set from scratch.

## API reference

### ReelSetBuilder
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/docs/api-builder/
Fluent builder for ReelSet. Reduces ~100 lines of manual wiring to ~10, validates everything at .build() time.

### Events
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/docs/api-events/
The full typed event surface. spin, cascade, pin, MultiWays, win, speed, spotlight.

### Phases
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/docs/api-phases/
START, SPIN, ANTICIPATION, STOP. the four lifecycle phases and how to extend them.

### ReelSet
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/docs/api-reelset/
The main orchestrator. Created via ReelSetBuilder, drives every spin, refill, pin, and win animation.

### Glossary
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/docs/glossary/
Plain-English definitions for every term you will see in pixi-reels docs, code, and slot-game conversations.

### Migrating to 1.0.0
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/docs/migrating-to-1-0/
Mechanical changes to move from 0.x to 1.0.0. Every breaking change with a before and after.

## Recipes

### How to anticipate a reel
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/anticipate-a-reel/
Slow a specific reel before it lands to build tension. the classic "hold" on reels 4/5 when 2 scatters are already visible.
Tags: anticipation, tension
APIs: ReelSet.setAnticipation, AnticipationPhase, spin:stopping event
Steps:
  - During a spin, decide which reels deserve anticipation
  - Call reelSet.setAnticipation([...reelIndices]) BEFORE calling setResult()
  - The marked reels will enter the ANTICIPATION phase before STOP

### Anticipation teaser
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/anticipation-teaser/
Pair setAnticipation with a buffer-above prefill so the next cell to scroll into view on a slow reel is a caller-chosen symbol.
Tags: anticipation, tension, buffer, peek
APIs: ReelSet.setAnticipation, ReelSet.setResult, AnticipationPhase
Steps:
  - Call setAnticipation([...reels]) to slow specific reels before they land
  - Pass a ColumnTarget with bufferAbove ['BONUS'] on those same reels so the symbol sits one slot above the visible window
  - During the anticipation decelerate, the buffer-above cell is the next to enter view

### Big symbol falls into view through a cascade
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/big-symbol-cascade-fall/
A tall wild lands with its tail peeking in from bufferAbove. The cells below the tail form a winning cluster, clear in the cascade, and the wild's anchor falls down through the strip into full visibility.
Tags: big-symbols, buffer, cascade, tumble, recent
APIs: ReelSet.runCascade, ReelSet.setResult, ColumnTarget.bufferAbove, SymbolData.size, ReelSetBuilder.tumble
Steps:
  - Land a 1xH wild with its anchor in `bufferAbove`. tail visible at row 0
  - Plant a winning cluster on a row below the tail so detectWinners returns those cells
  - In `nextGrid`, return a refill grid that places the wild's anchor at a more visible row. bufferAbove or visible
  - The cascade refill animates each strip cell, including the moved anchor sprite, into its new position

### Nudge a big symbol in, then hold it across a respin
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/big-symbol-held-respin/
A tall wild lands with its anchor in bufferAbove (tail visible at row 0). Nudge the trigger reel to drag the full block into view, then call spin with holdReels so the now-revealed wild survives a respin of the other reels.
Tags: big-symbols, buffer, hold-reels, respin, nudge
APIs: ReelSet.spin, SpinOptions.holdReels, ReelSet.setResult, ColumnTarget.bufferAbove, ReelSet.nudge
Steps:
  - Land a 1xH wild with its anchor in bufferAbove on the trigger reel. only the bottom cell of the block shows at row 0
  - Nudge the trigger reel down to drag the full block into visibility
  - Call reelSet.spin with holdReels including the trigger reel to skip START/SPIN/STOP on that column
  - Pass a full-width grid to setResult. the held column entry is ignored; the revealed block stays put

### Land a big symbol partially in buffer
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/big-symbol-partial-land/
A tall 1xH block lands with its anchor in bufferAbove. only the bottom cell shows at the top of the visible window. Nudge to drag the rest into view. Works end-to-end through setResult, refill, nudge, and the cross-reel resolver.
Tags: big-symbols, buffer, nudge, recent
APIs: ReelSet.setResult, SymbolData.size, ColumnTarget.bufferAbove, ColumnTarget.bufferBelow, ReelSet.getSymbolFootprint
Steps:
  - Pass an anchor at `bufferAbove[i]` on the column's `ColumnTarget`
  - Coordinator paints OCCUPIED at the rest of the block's cells (negative rows, visible rows, or bufferBelow as needed)
  - `_finalizeFrame` sizes the anchor sprite to span the whole block; the mask clips the off-screen portion
  - `getVisibleSymbols` resolves visible cells to the anchor id via a NEGATIVE `anchorRow` in `_occupancy`

### MxN big symbols. every shape
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/big-symbols-mxn/
Big symbols at any rectangular size. 2×2 squares, 1×3 tall bars, 3×3 giants, 2×4 wide banners. Same registration API for all four; each shape has its own use case. One interactive example per shape, with the placement logic spelled out.
Tags: big-symbols, layout, registration, sizing
APIs: SymbolData.size, ReelSet.getSymbolFootprint, ReelSet.getBlockBounds, validation: 'block exceeds reel height/count'
Steps:
  - Register each oversized symbol with size { w, h }. any positive integers
  - The server places the symbol id at the anchor (top-left) cell only
  - Engine paints OCCUPIED across the rest of the block before per-reel framebuilding

### Board primitive: build your own board
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/board-grid-reveal/
A reveal-and-collect prize grid built directly on the generic BoardGrid primitive — no HoldAndWinBuilder. Every cell spins independently to a result you choose; the rule (tally, find the top) is yours. Hold & Win is just one opinionated board on the same primitive.
Tags: board, primitive, boardgrid, build-your-own
APIs: BoardGrid (pixi-reels), BoardGrid.spinCells / cells / symbolAt / cellBounds, BlurSpriteSymbol
Steps:
  - new BoardGrid({ cols, rows, cellSize, symbols, ticker }) lays out a grid of independent reels
  - A per-cell profile function gives the diagonal stagger wave
  - spinCells(targets) spins each cell to a result YOU choose; await the whole wave
  - Your own rule tallies the prizes and pops the top cell via symbolAt + cellBounds

### CardSymbol. debug / prototyping helper
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/card-symbol-debug/
A no-asset PIXI.Graphics symbol class for recipes, mechanic tests, and prototypes. Always crisp at any cell size. NOT for production. ship SpriteSymbol, AnimatedSpriteSymbol, or SpineSymbol instead.
Tags: debug, prototyping, custom-symbol, graphics
APIs: CardSymbol, CARD_DECK, WILD_CARD, PIXI.Graphics
Steps:
  - Import `CardSymbol`, `CARD_DECK` (and optional `WILD_CARD`) from `examples/shared/CardSymbol.ts`
  - Register one symbol id per card with its own color
  - No textures, no atlases. geometry redrawn at every resize

### Cascade 6×5 tumble
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/cascade-6x5/
Modern tumble/cascade mechanic on the runCascade orchestrator. the MultiWays-adjacent style every slot studio is shipping.
Tags: starter, cascade, 6x5, multiplier
APIs: ReelSetBuilder.tumble, ReelSet.runCascade, ReelSet.destroySymbols, ReelSet.refill, ReelSet.setDropOrder
Steps:
  - Build a 6×5 ReelSet with .tumble({ fall, dropIn }) for drop-in mechanics
  - Spin. symbols drop in left-to-right, row by row, with bounce
  - reelSet.runCascade({ detectWinners, nextGrid }) owns the destroy → refill loop
  - onCascade fires per refill. bump the multiplier there
  - The awaited RunCascadeResult tells you the chain ended

### Cascade pops with WinPresenter
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/cascade-winpresenter/
Drive cascade cell pops with the same WinPresenter you use for paylines. One API, one shape.
Tags: wins, cascade
APIs: WinPresenter, ReelSet.runCascade (onCascade), win:group event
Steps:
  - Build a WinPresenter. no special cluster config, it already works
  - In runCascade's onCascade callback, hand winners to the presenter and await it
  - destroySymbols runs after. disable its zIndex lift so its alpha→0 stays invisible

### Cell bounds. overlays, paylines & hit areas
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/cell-bounds/
getCellBounds(col, row) returns the pixel rectangle of any visible cell. Use it to draw payline lines, win-box outlines, or invisible hit areas that align exactly with symbols.
Tags: utility, paylines, hit-areas, graphics
APIs: ReelSet.getCellBounds, CellBounds
Steps:
  - Call reelSet.getCellBounds(col, row) to get { x, y, width, height } in ReelSet-local coords
  - Draw a PIXI.Graphics outline or line using those coordinates
  - addChild the graphic to reelSet so it moves with the board

### Cell hit areas. click to pick, hover to preview
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/cell-hit-areas/
Use getCellBounds to attach pointer events to individual grid cells. cursor changes to a pointer on hover, click toggles a pick state, all cells stay selectable across spins.
Tags: utility, hit-areas, interaction, cursor
APIs: ReelSet.getCellBounds, PIXI.Graphics.eventMode, PIXI.Graphics.cursor
Steps:
  - For each cell call reelSet.getCellBounds(col, row)
  - Build an invisible PIXI.Graphics rect and set eventMode "static" + cursor "pointer
  - Attach pointerover / pointerout / pointertap listeners to drive the UI

### Classic 5×3 starter
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/classic-5x3/
Copy-paste starting point for a left-to-right line-pay slot. the foundation of 95% of slot games.
Tags: starter, 5x3, line-pays
APIs: ReelSetBuilder, SpriteSymbol, SpeedPresets, SymbolSpotlight.cycle
Steps:
  - Build a 5-reel × 3-row ReelSet with your sprite atlas
  - Handle spin + call spotlight.cycle on wins
  - Ship

### Beginner: carry the value as data
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/coin-value-data/
The same coin, but the number lives in data instead of being baked into the class. One generic coin symbol carries any value the server sends, painted from the coin:locked event. The Hold & Win way.
Tags: beginner, basics, coin, value, data, hold-and-win
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.events (coin:locked), HoldAndWinBoard.cellCenter
Steps:
  - Keep ONE generic coin symbol with no number baked in
  - Send the amount in the hit data — the coin id plus data carrying the value
  - The board stores data and never reads it
  - Paint the number yourself from the coin:locked event, positioned with cellCenter()

### Beginner: number on a ready-made coin
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/coin-value-overlay/
Don't draw the coin — use the artwork you already have (a sprite or Spine coin) and add the number on top. Your symbol's view holds two children, the art and a text label. The practical, production-style way.
Tags: beginner, basics, coin, value, composition, sprite
APIs: ReelSymbol (subclass it), loadHoldAndWinSprites (examples/shared), PIXI.Sprite / PIXI.BitmapText
Steps:
  - Put the ready-made coin art (a Sprite) in your symbol's view
  - Add a PIXI.BitmapText label as a second child, on top
  - Position and scale both in resize()
  - The label shows the value; the art does the rest

### Beginner: carry a value on a coin
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/coin-value-symbol/
The simplest thing — build your own symbol class that draws a coin with a number on it. Learn what a ReelSymbol is, the few methods the engine calls on it, and how to read the value back.
Tags: beginner, basics, symbol, coin, value, custom-symbol
APIs: ReelSymbol (subclass it), ReelSetBuilder.symbols / register, Reel.getSymbolAt
Steps:
  - A symbol is any subclass of ReelSymbol; the engine makes one per cell
  - Take the value in the constructor and store it
  - Draw the coin + number in resize() (it runs on every swap with the cell size)
  - Read the value back off the landed symbol instance any time

### Collector symbol (Hold & Win)
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/collector-symbol-pin/
On a HoldAndWinBuilder board, the collector orb absorbs its neighbours — walk board.lockedCoins, sum the adjacent coins, fly their values into the orb and release() those cells. No pins, no hand-rolled grid.
Tags: coin, collector, hold-and-win
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.lockedCoins / release / symbolAt, bezierFly (examples/shared)
Steps:
  - Build the board with HoldAndWinBuilder; coins carry data.value
  - When the collector locks, find its neighbours in board.lockedCoins
  - Fly each neighbour value into the orb and release() the cell
  - Show the absorbed total on the collector's badge

### EmptySymbol. render nothing for a registered id
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/empty-symbol/
Register an EmptySymbol against an id like 'empty' to reserve a grid slot that produces no visual output. Useful for hold-and-win mechanics, miss cells, and any board where blank is the intended visual.
Tags: symbols, empty, hold-and-win
APIs: ReelSymbol, SymbolRegistry.register, ReelSetBuilder.symbols, ReelSetBuilder.weights
Steps:
  - Register the empty id with EmptySymbol. `r.register('empty', EmptySymbol, {})`
  - Weight empty heavily so most cells land blank; weight real symbols lightly so they pop
  - The reel renders empty cells as zero pixels. no sprite, no graphic, no animation

### Expanding wild (sticky)
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/expanding-wild-pin/
When a wild lands, the whole column fills with wild and stays expanded for N spins. Built with CellPin numeric turns. auto-expires when the counter runs out.
Tags: wild, expanding, sticky, cell-pin
APIs: ReelSet.pin, CellPin.turns (number)
Steps:
  - On spin:allLanded, find every reel containing a wild
  - Pin every row of that reel with { turns: N }. the column becomes wild for the next N spins
  - The engine decrements turns on each spin:allLanded and expires pins at zero

### Lead-in delay. how long before the fall starts
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/fall-delays/
Four interactive canvases. Same spin, same destruction, same .tumble() feel. only the pause between SPIN click and the first frame of fall-out animation changes. From snappy 0 ms to dramatic 700 ms.
Tags: cascade, tumble, lead-in, pacing, recipes
APIs: ReelSet.spin, (user-code: await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms)))
Steps:
  - Click SPIN. your handler runs immediately
  - Optionally await a lead-in pause (0 / 150 / 350 / 700 ms here)
  - Call reelSet.spin(). the fall begins

### Feature mode swap
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/feature-mode-swap/
Toggle a bonus mode on and off at runtime by adding and removing a frame middleware. no ReelSet rebuild, no rebuild latency.
Tags: mode, feature, middleware, frame
APIs: ReelSet.frame.use, ReelSet.frame.remove, FrameMiddleware, FrameAPI
Steps:
  - Write a FrameMiddleware that rewrites frames for the bonus mode
  - On feature entry, call reelSet.frame.use(middleware)
  - On feature exit, call reelSet.frame.remove(name)
  - Change takes effect on the very next spin. no rebuild

### getBlockBounds. outline a big symbol
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/get-block-bounds/
Draw one overlay rectangle that hugs an entire N×M block, regardless of which cell you pass. Works for any size. 1×1 (same as getCellBounds), 1×N expanding wilds, 2×2 / 3×3 blocks.
Tags: big-symbols, overlay, cell-bounds
APIs: ReelSet.getBlockBounds, ReelSet.getSymbolFootprint
Steps:
  - getBlockBounds(col, row) returns { x, y, width, height } in ReelSet-local pixels
  - Pass any cell of a block. anchor or non-anchor. both return the same rect
  - Use for win frames, cluster outlines, hit-area overlays around big symbols

### Hold & Win respin
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win/
Coins lock on land; every new coin resets the respin counter; fill the grid for the grand jackpot. The "coin" formula every studio is shipping in 2024.
Tags: starter, hold-and-win, respin, jackpot
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.enter / respin / reset, HoldAndWinBoard.events, HoldAndWinBoard.cellCenter / symbolAt
Steps:
  - Build the board once — grid, coin symbols, strip weights, respin count
  - enter(seed) with the triggering coins, then await respin(hits) per round
  - Board events drive the HUD — respins:changed, coin:locked, board:full
  - board:full fires the GRAND when the grid fills; reset() clears the board for the next round

### Hold & Win: last-cell anticipation
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-anticipation/
One builder knob — anticipateWhen(({ locked, capacity }) => capacity - locked === 1) — flips the last still-spinning cell to a drawn-out tension profile, the "one more for the full-board jackpot" moment. The recipe only listens to events.
Tags: hold-and-win, anticipation, tension, respin, spine
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder.anticipateWhen, HoldAndWinBoard.events (respin:start, cell:landed), HoldAndWinBoard.isFull / capacity
Steps:
  - Build with anticipateWhen(({ locked, capacity }) => capacity - locked === 1)
  - When one cell is left, the board runs it on the 'tension' speed profile
  - respin:start tells you which cells spin — pulse the last one and update the HUD
  - On a full board, board:full / isFull report the jackpot

### Hold & Win: enter from the base game and back
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-base-to-feature/
A normal 5x3 spin that, when 3 BONUS land, transitions in the same chain into the Hold & Win board — every cell now its own reel — runs the feature, and swaps back to the base game on feature:end. Two display objects, one Spin button.
Tags: hold-and-win, base-game, transition, feature, trigger
APIs: ReelSetBuilder (base game), HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.enter / events (feature:enter, feature:end)
Steps:
  - Spin a normal ReelSet; count the BONUS symbols that land
  - On a 3-BONUS trigger, hide the base reels and reveal board.container
  - enter(seed) carries the trigger coins into the feature already locked
  - On feature:end, fade the board out and show the base game again

### Hold & Win: bonus cells
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-bonus-cell/
A few cells are marked as bonus cells from the start; a coin that lands on one flashes the cell active and doubles its value. Positional special cells are pure game state — the board only reports where each coin landed.
Tags: hold-and-win, bonus-cell, positional, sprites
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.events (coin:locked) / cellCenter, loadHoldAndWinSprites (examples/shared)
Steps:
  - Mark a set of cells as bonus cells and draw a glowing marker on each
  - On coin:locked, check whether the coin's cell is a bonus cell
  - If so, flash the marker active, fade it out and double that coin's value
  - Repaint the boosted label — the board is untouched

### Hold & Win: drive each cell on its own
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-cell-control/
Every cell is its own 1x1 ReelSet, so board.reelAt(cell) hands you the raw reel — start it, slam-stop it, read the symbol inside it, independently of every other cell.
Tags: hold-and-win, cell, reel, control, start-stop
APIs: HoldAndWinBoard.reelAt, HoldAndWinBoard.symbolAt, ReelSet.spin / skipSpin / isSpinning
Steps:
  - board.reelAt(cell) returns that cell's ReelSet
  - Start cells on their own clocks (staggered) — each is independent
  - Slam-stop a single cell with reelAt(cell).skipSpin() while others spin
  - Read the live symbol with symbolAt(cell)

### Hold & Win: collector + flight choreography
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-collector/
A board that opens holding three 5.00 coins, lands a 10.00, then lands a collector orb that pulls a value clone out of every coin along bezier arcs — summing up tick by tick. Next press resets with a column-wave sweep.
Tags: hold-and-win, collector, spine, flight, choreography
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), coinWaves / bezierFly (examples/shared/holdAndWinFx), HoldAndWinBoard.reset / cellCenter / symbolAt, GoldCoinSymbol
Steps:
  - The board opens pre-seeded — enter(seed) runs at boot, before any spin
  - Each respin spins only the free cells; held coins sit out
  - The collector lands; value-label clones bezierFly() into it, the sum ticks up per arrival
  - coinWaves() picks the choreography (sequence / by-row / by-col / all / chunks)
  - No respin starts until the previous wave's land animations resolve
  - The next press sweeps the board out by column and reseeds the opening state

### Hold & Win: collector with particle streams
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-collector-particles/
A collector lands and each coin's value flies into it on a bezier arc trailed by a stream of neon particles, ticking the collector total up. Pure game-layer choreography over the board's cellCenter() and release().
Tags: hold-and-win, collector, particles, sprites, collect
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.cellCenter / release / symbolAt, bezierFly + coinWaves (examples/shared)
Steps:
  - The board opens holding value coins; a collector lands (server-placed)
  - For each coin, fly the value into the collector on a bezier arc
  - Trail each flight with a stream of neon particle sprites
  - release() the cell and tick the collector total on arrival

### Hold & Win: count-up coin
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-countup-coin/
Each coin's amount ticks up from 0.00 to its value as it settles, in a left-to-right wave, instead of snapping to the number. The count is a plain gsap tween that rewrites the gold-font label every frame.
Tags: hold-and-win, coins, count-up, spine
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.cellCenter / lockedCoins, coinWaves (examples/shared)
Steps:
  - Land a coin and paint a 0.00 label on its face
  - Tween a { v } counter to coin.data.value, rewriting the label every frame
  - Pop the final width-fitted amount in when the count finishes
  - coinWaves orders the count-ups into a reading-order wave

### Hold & Win: fly to a feature meter
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-meter/
On collect, each coin's value flies UP out of its cell, shrinks into a meter widget above the reels (arriveScale), ticks the meter total up and fills a progress bar toward a target. The board hands out geometry; the flight is yours.
Tags: hold-and-win, coins, meter, collect, flight, spine
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.cellCenter / release, bezierFly + coinWaves (examples/shared)
Steps:
  - Place a meter widget (spine counter + progress bar + total) above the board
  - On collect, fly each coin's value from cellCenter() up to the meter with arriveScale
  - On arrival, add to the total, fill the bar and tick the counter animation
  - release() each cell as its coin leaves

### Hold & Win: multiplier-strike coin
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-multiplier/
A multiplier coin lands, the strike FX (17-frame AnimatedSprite) fires over it, a ×N badge pops in the game's multiplier font, and every coin's value is multiplied. The multiplier lives in coin.data — the board never reads it.
Tags: hold-and-win, multiplier, sprites, fx, coins
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), loadHoldAndWinSprites (examples/shared), HoldAndWinBoard.lockedCoins / symbolAt, PIXI.AnimatedSprite
Steps:
  - Land a multiplier coin via the board (server-placed, carries data.mult)
  - After the coin lands, play the strike FX AnimatedSprite over the cell and pop a ×N badge
  - Multiply every value coin's data.value and bump its label
  - The board stays value-blind — the multiply is pure game state

### Hold & Win: mystery value coin
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-mystery-coin/
A coin lands as a blank gold disc and its amount is unknown until, on lock, a strip of candidate values spins inside the coin face and decelerates onto the real number. The value lives in coin.data; the board never reads it.
Tags: hold-and-win, coins, mystery, reveal, spine
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), GoldCoinSymbol (examples/shared), HoldAndWinBoard.symbolAt / cellCenter, HoldAndWinBoard.events (coin:locked)
Steps:
  - Coins land settled on the gold money face with no number painted yet
  - On coin:locked, scroll a masked strip of candidate amounts inside the coin and ease it onto the real value
  - Paint the final amount in the gold bitmap font and play the coin flourish as the "ding
  - Gate the next respin on the pending reveals so nothing lands mid-roll

### Hold & Win: payer / doubler coin
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-payer-coin/
A special payer orb lands and pumps its value into every other coin on the board — a gold token arcs to each coin in turn and bumps its amount on arrival. The same pattern doubles every value with one line changed.
Tags: hold-and-win, coins, payer, collector, spine
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.lockedCoins / symbolAt / cellCenter, bezierFly + coinWaves (examples/shared), SpineReelSymbol (pixi-reels/spine)
Steps:
  - Open the board holding value coins; the payer is a server-placed id (weight 0)
  - After the payer lands, walk board.lockedCoins and fly a gold token to each value coin
  - On arrival, bump that coin's data.value, repaint the label and flash the coin
  - The board stays value-blind — the bump is pure game state

### Hold & Win: row-complete jackpot
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-row-jackpot/
When a coin completes a full row, that row flashes coin by coin, the MINI plaque above the board fires, and the row's summed value flies up into it. Row detection is pure game logic over board.lockedCoins — the board has no notion of paying rows.
Tags: hold-and-win, coins, jackpot, row, plaque, spine
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.events (coin:locked) / lockedCoins / symbolAt, bezierFly + coinWaves (examples/shared)
Steps:
  - On coin:locked, check whether the new coin completed a full row
  - If it did, flash the row coin by coin and fire the MINI plaque's win
  - Sum the row and fly the total up into the plaque
  - Gate the next press until the jackpot choreography settles

### Hold & Win: skip the whole feature
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-skip/
board.skip() slams every in-flight cell to its landed position and fires feature:skip; the game layer cuts its own flights and collect short. feature:end stays the single "feature over" signal.
Tags: hold-and-win, skip, fast-forward, events
APIs: HoldAndWinBoard.skip, HoldAndWinBoard.events (feature:skip, feature:end), RunResult.onSkip (RecipeRunner)
Steps:
  - Tap the button mid-feature — RecipeRunner routes it to onSkip
  - onSkip calls board.skip() to slam every in-flight spin (fires feature:skip)
  - The game layer cuts its own flights / collect short on the same signal
  - feature:end fires as the single "feature over" event

### Hold & Win: Spine coins + collect
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-spine/
A 5x4 Hold & Win board where every cell is a real Spine skeleton, with MINI / MAJOR jackpot coins whose tier one-shot IS the reveal, side plaques that celebrate, and a collect flight into a Spine counter.
Tags: hold-and-win, spine, collect, respin, jackpot
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), SpineReelSymbol (pixi-reels/spine), HoldAndWinBoard.release / cellCenter, HoldAndWinBoard.events
Steps:
  - Register each id against its skeleton via a spineMap (the jackpot coin uses a small custom class for its tier reveal)
  - Jackpot coins land wearing their tier word; on lock the reveal spins it off into the money face
  - coin:locked paints value labels and flashes the matching MINI / MAJOR side plaque
  - On feature end, release() each cell and fly a Spine clone to the counter via cellCenter()

### Hold & Win: sprite coins (no Spine)
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-sprites/
The Hold & Win board built entirely from TexturePacker sprites — number symbols that blur as they spin (BlurSpriteSymbol) and a 30-frame coin flip on land (AnimatedSpriteSymbol). The sprite path that ships most often, no skeleton runtime.
Tags: hold-and-win, sprites, animated-sprite, blur, coins
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), loadHoldAndWinSprites (examples/shared), BlurSpriteSymbol / AnimatedSpriteSymbol
Steps:
  - loadHoldAndWinSprites() returns the base + motion-blur textures, the coin frames and the value font
  - Register number symbols on BlurSpriteSymbol so they swap to blur on spin and crisp on land
  - Register the coin on AnimatedSpriteSymbol — the board's playWin() plays the flip
  - Everything else is the same HoldAndWinBuilder board as the Spine recipes

### Hold & Win: an overlay that survives swaps and rides flights
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-sticky-overlay/
A ×100 badge lives in coin.data and is attached as a child of the symbol's view, so it survives an in-place symbol swap (re-attached from data) and travels with the coin on a collect flight (rebuilt onto the flying clone).
Tags: hold-and-win, overlay, badge, swap, flight
APIs: HoldAndWinBoard.symbolAt / setSymbolAt, HoldAndWinBoard.cellCenter / release, bezierFly (examples/shared)
Steps:
  - Keep the overlay value in coin.data (badge, jackpot label)
  - Attach it as a CHILD of symbolAt(cell).view so it rides with the symbol
  - On an in-place swap, re-attach from coin.data (the new instance has a fresh view)
  - On a flight, rebuild it onto the flying clone from the same data

### Hold & Win: swap a symbol in place (coin → MINI → MAJOR)
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-tier-swap/
board.setSymbolAt(cell, id, data) re-places one cell with a different symbol — a coin climbs the jackpot tiers — and rewrites that cell's ledger entry, leaving every other coin exactly where it was.
Tags: hold-and-win, swap, jackpot, tier, upgrade, spine
APIs: HoldAndWinBoard.setSymbolAt, HoldAndWinBoard.symbolAt, SpineReelSymbol (pixi-reels/spine)
Steps:
  - Register the plain coin and the MINI / MAJOR jackpot variants (same skeleton, tier-word idles)
  - On each press, board.setSymbolAt(cell, tierId, data) swaps the art in place
  - The cell's ledger entry is rewritten so lockedCoins / totals stay correct
  - Neighbouring coins are untouched

### Hold & Win: live event trace
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-trace/
A small board runs a full feature while every board.events beat prints to a live log — feature:enter, respin:start, cell:landed, coin:locked, respins:changed, respin:end, board:full / feature:end. Watch the lifecycle instead of imagining it.
Tags: hold-and-win, events, lifecycle, debugging
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.events, HoldAndWinBoard.phase
Steps:
  - Build a plain HoldAndWinBuilder board and subscribe to every event
  - enter(seed) opens the feature; a respin loop drives waves until done
  - Each event appends a colour-coded line to an on-canvas log, in fire order
  - Nothing touches board internals — the trace only listens

### Hold & Win: upgrade a coin in place
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/hold-and-win-upgrade-coin/
A held coin climbs the value ladder (5 → 10 → 25 → 50 → 100) with a flip-and-pop flourish — no respin, no cell movement. The new tier lives in coin.data; the label is just repainted.
Tags: hold-and-win, coins, upgrade, transform, spine
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.symbolAt / lockedCoins, GoldCoinSymbol (examples/shared)
Steps:
  - Open the board holding coins at the lowest tier
  - On each press, bump every held coin one rung up the value ladder
  - Play the coin's one-turn flourish and pop the new amount in
  - The board is untouched — only coin.data and the label change

### Multiplier wild
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/multiplier-wild-pin/
Each wild carries a per-instance multiplier value in its pin payload. Game layer reads the payload during win evaluation to scale the payout.
Tags: wild, multiplier, payload, cell-pin
APIs: ReelSet.pin, CellPin.payload, pin:placed event
Steps:
  - Pin wilds on land with a payload containing the multiplier value
  - Draw a ×N badge on each pinned cell via pin:placed event
  - On wins, read pin.payload.multiplier to scale the payout

### MultiWays
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/multiways/
Per-spin row variation. Each reel lands on a different row count between minRows and maxRows. The reel pixel height stays fixed; cell heights are derived per spin. Total ways = product of per-reel rows.
Tags: multiways, shape, geometry, reshape, variable-rows
APIs: ReelSetBuilder.multiways, ReelSet.setShape, AdjustPhase, events: shape:changed, adjust:start, adjust:complete
Steps:
  - Build with .multiways({ minRows, maxRows, reelPixelHeight }). defines the height budget
  - Each spin, pick a per-reel row count and call reelSet.setShape(rowsPerReel) BEFORE setResult()
  - AdjustPhase commits the new geometry; cell symbols snap, pin overlays tween
Real game example: The Dog House (Pragmatic Play) (https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/videos/dog-house-megaways.mp4)

### MultiWays cascade
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/multiways-cascade/
Per-spin row variation on a strip-spin landing; the cascade tumble pops winners and drops new symbols in from above. Shape-aware on every reel.
Tags: multiways, cascade, hybrid, recent
APIs: ReelSetBuilder.multiways, ReelSetBuilder.tumble, ReelSet.setShape, ReelSet.runCascade
Steps:
  - Build with .multiways({minRows, maxRows, reelPixelHeight}) + .tumble({...}) for the refill phases
  - Each round, call setShape(rowsPerReel) BEFORE setResult()
  - reelSet.spin({ mode: 'standard' }). classic strip-spin lands the multiways grid (AdjustPhase reshapes during SPIN→STOP)
  - reelSet.runCascade({ detectWinners, nextGrid }) tumbles winners and drops new symbols in from above

### Mystery reveal (CellPin)
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/mystery-reveal-pin/
Mystery symbols land, then all reveal to the same random class via 'eval' pins. Auto-cleared at next spin.
Tags: mystery, reveal, cell-pin
APIs: ReelSet.pin, CellPin.turns ('eval')
Steps:
  - Server places 'mystery' symbols at specific cells
  - On spin:allLanded, pick one random class and pin all mystery cells with turns 'eval
  - Pins auto-clear on next spin:start

### How to fake a near-miss
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/near-miss/
Place N-1 scatters, add anticipation on the reel that "almost" landed one. Stops short of a scatter trigger while the slow reel decelerates.
Tags: anticipation, near-miss
APIs: ReelSet.setAnticipation, ReelSet.setResult, forceNearMiss cheat
Steps:
  - Build a result grid with count - 1 scatters, none on the near reel
  - setAnticipation([nearReelIndex]) BEFORE setResult()
  - Let the slow reel decelerate on the last column

### How to nudge a reel
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/nudge/
Shift one reel down or up by N positions after it has landed, with the reveal symbols supplied by the caller.
Tags: nudge, recent
APIs: ReelSet.nudge, NudgeOptions, nudge:start, nudge:complete
Steps:
  - Wait for spin() to land
  - Call reelSet.nudge(col, { distance, direction, incoming }). incoming is required, top-down order of the new visible positions
  - Await the promise to read the new visible column for re-running win detection
  - For multi-reel beats, wrap several nudge() calls in Promise.all([...])

### Abort a nudge
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/nudge-abort/
Cancel a running nudge via AbortSignal. the strip still snaps to its deterministic landed position, but the `nudge()` promise rejects with `AbortError` and `nudge:cancelled` fires on the bus.
Tags: nudge, abort, cancellation, signal, recent
APIs: NudgeOptions.signal, AbortController, nudge:cancelled
Steps:
  - Wire an `AbortController` into `NudgeOptions.signal`
  - Trigger the abort while the tween is running
  - Catch `AbortError` to take the cancellation path; non-abort errors re-throw
  - `nudge:cancelled` fires on the bus carrying the cancellation reason

### Nudge through a big symbol
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/nudge-big-symbol/
A 1xH block on the target reel is nudged through as a unit. The engine survival-checks anchor + h fits on the strip post-rotation; cross-reel (w>1) blocks still throw.
Tags: nudge, big-symbols, block, recent
APIs: ReelSet.nudge, SymbolData.size
Steps:
  - Land with a 1x2 MEGA wild at column 2, rows 0+1
  - Nudge column 2 DOWN by 2. block survives (`1 + 2 - 1 + 2 < 5`) and anchor lands at row 2 with stub in bufferBelow
  - Nudge column 2 UP by 1. block returns to rows 1+2, fully visible
  - The whole block moves as a unit; the wrap mechanism never splits anchor from stubs

### Skip an in-flight nudge
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/nudge-skip/
Fast-forward a running nudge tween to its landed state with `reelSet.skipNudge(col)`. The original `nudge()` promise resolves normally. the consumer's success path still runs.
Tags: nudge, skip, ux, recent
APIs: ReelSet.skipNudge, Reel.isNudging
Steps:
  - Run a long sequential nudge (1.5s per reel) so the player has time to react
  - The canvas button morphs into a skip icon while the recipe handler is running
  - Tap during a nudge. the RecipeRunner detects an in-flight nudge and calls `reelSet.skipNudge()`
  - The original `nudge()` promise resolves at the deterministic landed position

### Spotlight after a nudge
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/nudge-spotlight/
Land a near-miss, nudge three reels down by one to complete a wild line across the top row, then run SymbolSpotlight on the new winners. Chains nudge() and spotlight.show() in their canonical order.
Tags: nudge, spotlight, wins, recent
APIs: ReelSet.nudge, ReelSet.spotlight.show, ReelSet.spotlight.hide, nudge:complete
Steps:
  - Spin and land on a flat result with no wilds anywhere
  - Promise.all three `reelSet.nudge(col, ...)` calls so the middle reels drop in `wild` at row 0 together
  - Build the winning cells (row 0 on each nudged reel) and call `reelSet.spotlight.show(cells, ...)`
  - Await spotlight completion, then `spotlight.hide()` to return the symbols to the grid

### Staggered nudge. wave reveal
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/nudge-stagger/
Use `NudgeOptions.startDelay` inside a `Promise.all([...])` to dispatch parallel nudges with a per-reel offset. Total wall time = last reel's delay + duration.
Tags: nudge, stagger, wave, ux, recent
APIs: ReelSet.nudge, NudgeOptions.startDelay
Steps:
  - Map over the target reels with `Promise.all(cols.map(...))`
  - Pass `startDelay: i * STAGGER_MS` per call so each reel waits a beat longer to begin
  - All tweens run concurrently. total wall time = last reel's delay + duration
  - Validation throws still fire synchronously; only the mutation is deferred

### Custom per-symbol animation
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/paylines-custom-animation/
Skip playWin(). drive each cell with a GSAP timeline, styled per win.id.
Tags: wins, gsap
APIs: WinPresenter.symbolAnim, WinSymbolAnim type
Steps:
  - Pass a `symbolAnim` callback to WinPresenter
  - Receive (symbol, cell, win) and return a Promise. the presenter awaits it
  - Style per win by switching on `win.id` / `win.kind`

### Draw paylines yourself from win:group
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/paylines-events-only/
The presenter never draws lines. Subscribe to win:group and use reelSet.getCellBounds to render any overlay.
Tags: wins, events, overlays
APIs: ReelSet.getCellBounds, win:group / win:symbol / win:end events
Steps:
  - Create a WinPresenter. it only animates symbols
  - Subscribe to `win:group`, draw a line (or outline, or number) from `reelSet.getCellBounds`
  - Drop your own GSAP fade in + out so visuals don't pile up between wins

### Peek symbol from buffer-above
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/peek-from-above/
Pre-fill the buffer-above slot so a teaser symbol sits just out of frame. visible during scroll-in on the next spin.
Tags: buffer, peek, tension, reveal
APIs: ReelSetBuilder.initialFrame, ReelSet.setResult, ColumnTarget
Steps:
  - Seed initialFrame with a ColumnTarget so the buffer-above slot is set at build time
  - On each spin, pass the same shape to setResult so the teaser carries forward
  - When the next spin starts, that symbol is the first to scroll into the visible area

### Positional multiplier cells
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/positional-multiplier-pin/
Specific grid positions carry a multiplier. Any symbol landing on those cells boosts its own win contribution. Gonzo's Quest multiplier-reel style.
Tags: multiplier, positional, payload, cell-pin
APIs: ReelSet.pin, CellPin.payload, CellPin.turns ('eval')
Steps:
  - Mark fixed cells as multiplier positions (or receive them per-spin from server)
  - Draw persistent ×N badges at those cells
  - After landing, re-pin the rolled symbol with multiplier in payload (turns 'eval')
  - Win evaluator reads pin.payload.multiplier for any winning cell

### Per-reel shape (pyramid)
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/pyramid-shape/
Build a non-uniform reel set. 3-5-5-5-3 pyramid, diamond, half-pyramid. Static shape, set at build time, no per-spin reshaping.
Tags: shape, pyramid, geometry, layout
APIs: ReelSetBuilder.visibleRowsPerReel, ReelSetBuilder.reelAnchor, Reel.offsetY
Steps:
  - Pass an array to .visibleRowsPerReel([3, 5, 5, 5, 3]) instead of .visibleRows(n)
  - Optionally set .reelAnchor('center' | 'top' | 'bottom') to control vertical alignment
  - The engine computes per-reel offsetY automatically; getCellBounds(col, row) reflects it

### Cascade refill orders. pick the post-win flow
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/refill-orders/
Three interactive canvases. Same scripted win, same destruction, same .tumble() feel. only the cascade-refill ordering changes. See which one fits your slot's rhythm.
Tags: cascade, tumble, refill, presets, recipes
APIs: ReelSet.refill, ReelSet.setDropOrder, TumbleDropInConfig.rowStagger, TumbleDropInConfig.rowOrder
Steps:
  - Initial reveal is the same (left-to-right wave) on every canvas
  - Winners destruct the same way. the post-removal pause is fixed
  - Only the refill drop-in order changes (setDropOrder + rowStagger + rowOrder)

### How to remove a symbol in a cascade
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/remove-symbol/
Fade the winners out, then drop the next-stage symbols in from above. a proper cascade with no reel respin.
Tags: cascade, animation
APIs: ReelSet.destroySymbols, ReelSet.refill, ReelSet.runCascade
Steps:
  - Detect winners on the visible grid (game-rule callback)
  - reelSet.destroySymbols(winners) fades them out
  - reelSet.refill({ winners, grid }) slides survivors down and drops new symbols in from above
  - reelSet.runCascade({ detectWinners, nextGrid }) wires all three together for a multi-cascade chain

### How to slam-stop
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/slam-stop/
Let the player tap the spin button again to land the reels immediately. `requestSkip()` queues until the result is in, no race-window to manage.
Tags: skip, UX, recent
APIs: ReelSet.requestSkip, ReelSet.skip, ReelSet.isSpinning, SpinResult.wasSkipped
Steps:
  - Bind the player tap to reelSet.requestSkip()
  - The engine waits until setResult() has fired, then slams
  - Inspect result.wasSkipped to adjust animations (shorter win celebration, etc.)

### Spin first, cascade after
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/spin-then-cascade/
Open every play with a classic strip-spin. The landing's winning cells pop, survivors fall down, new symbols drop in from above. repeat for every cascade in the chain.
Tags: hybrid, cascade, runCascade, recent
APIs: reelSet.spin, ReelSet.runCascade, ReelSet.destroySymbols, ReelSet.refill
Steps:
  - Round 1: a normal spin lands the initial grid
  - reelSet.runCascade({ detectWinners, nextGrid }) owns the pop-fall-refill loop for every chain stage
  - The awaited promise resolves with RunCascadeResult when no more winners are found

### Big symbols with Spine
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/spine-big-symbols/
Production-shape big-symbols recipe. a 2x2 wild placed at an anchor cell, with each cell a Spine skeleton that idles, lands, and plays a win animation. Same engine mechanic as the CardSymbol version, real art on top.
Tags: big-symbols, spine, custom-symbol, animation
APIs: SpineReelSymbol, loadGeneratedSpines, buildSpineMap, ReelSetBuilder.symbolData
Steps:
  - `await loadGeneratedSpines()` once before building. boots the atlas + 10 skeleton JSONs
  - Map your symbol ids to skeleton names with `buildSpineMap({ id: 'low_a', ... })`
  - Register every id with `SpineReelSymbol` and pass the same map to each registration
  - Mark the big symbol with `size: { w: 2, h: 2 }` and `weight: 0`. anchor-only, never from random fill

### Pyramid shape with Spine
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/spine-pyramid-shape/
Per-reel static shape (3-5-5-5-3 pyramid) rendered with Spine skeletons. The outer 3-row reels get taller cells than the inner 5-row reels. Spine scales each rig cleanly without the texture stretching you'd see with sprites.
Tags: shape, pyramid, spine, custom-symbol, animation
APIs: SpineReelSymbol, loadGeneratedSpines, buildSpineMap, ReelSetBuilder.visibleRowsPerReel, ReelSetBuilder.reelAnchor
Steps:
  - `await loadGeneratedSpines()` once before building
  - Use `.visibleRowsPerReel([3, 5, 5, 5, 3])` to declare the jagged shape
  - Register every id with `SpineReelSymbol`. same registration as a uniform layout

### Sticky wild on MultiWays
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/sticky-wild-multiways/
Pin survives every MultiWays reshape. Wild stays at its originRow when the shape allows; clamps to the last row when the shape shrinks; migrates back when the shape grows again.
Tags: multiways, sticky, wild, cell-pin, pin-migration
APIs: ReelSet.pin, CellPin.originRow, ReelSet.setShape, events: pin:migrated
Steps:
  - Pin wilds on spin:allLanded with { turns: N }. originRow defaults to current row
  - Each spin, setShape(rowsPerReel) before setResult(). setShape migrates pins eagerly
  - When new shape can't fit originRow, pin clamps; when it can again, pin restores

### Sticky wild (CellPin)
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/sticky-wild-pin/
Sticky wild using the engine's CellPin primitive. no ghost sprites, no manual grid injection. Pin once on land, the engine handles the rest.
Tags: wild, respin, sticky, cell-pin
APIs: ReelSet.pin, ReelSet.getPin, pin:expired event
Steps:
  - Listen for wilds landing via spin:allLanded
  - Call reelSet.pin(col, row, 'wild', { turns: N })
  - The engine overlays the pin at setResult and decrements turns automatically

### Sticky-win respin
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/sticky-win-respin-pin/
Winning symbols lock for N respins while the rest of the grid spins independently. the Dead or Alive II / Razor Shark pattern.
Tags: respin, sticky, win, cell-pin
APIs: ReelSet.pin, CellPin.turns (number), pin:expired event
Steps:
  - Detect winners on spin:allLanded
  - Pin each winner with turns N. N is the respin window
  - Non-winners respin naturally on the next spin, winners stay pinned
  - Pins decrement after each landing and expire automatically

### Symbol transform
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/symbol-transform/
A symbol morphs into a different (usually higher-paying) one mid-round. after a win, on a timer, or when a condition is met. Common in cluster-pays and upgrade-style slots.
Tags: transform, animation, upgrade
APIs: ReelSet.setSymbolAt, Reel.getSymbolAt, ReelSymbol.playWin
Steps:
  - Detect the transform trigger (a win, a cascade level, an RNG roll)
  - Tween the old symbol out (scale / fade / disintegrate)
  - Swap the symbol via reel.placeSymbols with the new identity
  - Tween the new symbol in

### How to load sprite symbols from a TexturePacker atlas
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/texture-atlas-symbols/
One atlas file, 80+ frames, zero per-image HTTP requests. Plus the separate-images fallback when you don't want to pack.
Tags: sprites, atlas, texturepacker
APIs: PIXI.Assets.load, Spritesheet.textures, ReelSetBuilder.symbols, SpriteSymbol, BlurSpriteSymbol
Steps:
  - Load the TexturePacker JSON with `PIXI.Assets.load()`. PixiJS understands the format natively
  - The returned Spritesheet exposes `sheet.textures[frameId]` for every frame
  - Pass the base + blur texture maps to `SpriteSymbol` (crisp) or `BlurSpriteSymbol` (swaps on phase events)

### Cascade anticipation refill. gravity, hold, column wave
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/tumble-anticipation/
Split the cascade refill in two. Survivors slide down to fill the holes first, a global anticipation beat for multipliers and mascots, then new symbols drop in column by column. First-class on pixi-reels.
Tags: cascade, tumble, refill, two-stage, anticipation, recipes
APIs: ReelSet.refill, ReelSet.runCascade, ReelSet.setDropOrder, RunCascadeOptions.refillMode, RunCascadeOptions.gravityHoldMs, RunCascadeOptions.onGravityComplete
Steps:
  - Initial reveal is a normal left-to-right wave
  - Winners destruct, then survivors slide down (cascade:gravity:* events fire)
  - Global gravityHoldMs window. plug anticipation visuals in here
  - New symbols drop in from above, column by column (cascade:dropIn:* events)

### Tumble feels. every preset, side by side
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/tumble-feels/
Five interactive canvases, same spin + cascade-refill loop. Only the .tumble({ fall, dropIn }) config changes between them. Click each SPIN to compare the feel without writing a line of code.
Tags: cascade, tumble, presets, recipes
APIs: ReelSetBuilder.tumble, ReelSet.refill, TumbleFallConfig, TumbleDropInConfig
Steps:
  - Same builder, same spin + refill loop on every canvas
  - Only the .tumble({ fall, dropIn }) shape differs
  - Pick the preset that reads right for your art / theme / pace

### Value-carrying coin (Hold & Win)
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/value-coin-pin/
Each coin carries a coefficient (×2 … ×50) in coin.data; HoldAndWinBuilder locks hits and respins only the free cells natively — no pins, no hand-rolled grid. The ×N badge is painted from data, the running total summed from board.lockedCoins.
Tags: coin, hold-and-win, value, data
APIs: HoldAndWinBuilder (pixi-reels), HoldAndWinBoard.respin / lockedCoins, HoldAndWinBoard.events (coin:locked)
Steps:
  - Build the board with HoldAndWinBuilder; coins are opaque { cell, id, data }
  - Each hit carries its coefficient in data.value
  - Paint the ×N badge from data on coin:locked
  - Sum the running total from board.lockedCoins

### Walking wild (movePin)
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/walking-wild-pin/
Walking wild migrating one column left each spin via reelSet.movePin(). no ghost sprites. With Spine-ready flight hooks for running/walking animations.
Tags: wild, walking, movement, spine, cell-pin
APIs: ReelSet.pin, ReelSet.movePin, ReelSet.unpin, MovePinOptions.onFlightCreated, pin:overlayCreated event, pin:moved event
Steps:
  - Pin new wilds on land with turns: 'permanent'
  - Before each spin, movePin() each existing pin one column left
  - When a pin reaches column 0, unpin(). it walked off the board
  - Use onFlightCreated / onFlightCompleted to drive Spine animations

## Mechanic demos

### Anticipation & slam-stop
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/demos/anticipation-slam/
Two tension tools. anticipation (slow a reel to tease a trigger) and skip (player smashes the button to slam-land now).
Tags: anticipation, skip

### Big symbols (2x2 BIG WILD)
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/demos/big-symbols/
Server places a single anchor cell, the engine paints the rest of the block with OCCUPIED stubs that resolve back to the anchor's id at evaluation. Glyph-only win animation keeps the cell border still while the character pulses.
Tags: big-symbols, wild, lines

### Cascade + multiplier
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/demos/cascade-multiplier/
A tumble slot. wins vanish, the next stage lands. Built around reelSet.runCascade(), which loops detect → destroy → refill until no more winners are found.
Tags: cascade, tumble, multiplier

### Classic line pays
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/demos/classic-lines/
The foundation of most slots. Detect 3+ matching symbols left-to-right, then spotlight each winning line.
Tags: basics, lines, spotlight

### Hold & Win respin
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/demos/hold-and-win-respin/
Every "coin" symbol locks. Respin until no new coins land or the grid fills. Plus a mini-jackpot at 3 coins on middle row.
Tags: hold-and-win, respin, jackpot

### MultiWays (Megaways)
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/demos/multiways/
Every spin rolls a fresh per-reel shape (2-7 rows each). The engine's AdjustPhase tweens cell heights between SPIN and STOP so the reels reshape mid-flight. Ways evaluation - chain of consecutive reels containing the kind, multiplied by per-reel match count.
Tags: megaways, multiways, multiways-ways

### Pyramid cascade (3-5-5-5-3)
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/demos/pyramid-cascade/
A diamond-shaped 3-5-5-5-3 reel set built with visibleRowsPerReel + center anchor. Cascade refills are real gravity - survivors fall only as far as the cleared slots beneath them, symbols with no cleared slots below them don't move at all.
Tags: pyramid, cascade, ways, gravity

### Scatter triggers Free Spins
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/demos/scatter-triggers-fs/
Land three scatters anywhere on the grid, play their win animation, transition to Free Spins. Plus a near-miss cheat for tension.
Tags: scatter, free-spins, anticipation

### Classic lines with sprite symbols
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/demos/sprite-classic/
Real sprite art on a classic 5×3 reel set. Symbols load from a single TexturePacker atlas (WebP + JSON), swap to pre-rendered motion-blur variants during the SPIN phase, and pulse on win.
Tags: sprites, atlas, blur-on-spin, classic-lines

### Sticky wilds
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/demos/sticky-wilds/
A wild lands and stays for the next 3 spins. New wilds reset the timer on that cell.
Tags: wild, sticky

## Recipe source code

Each recipe page on the site renders one of the following TypeScript files. Inlining the source here lets an LLM read working code without a follow-up fetch.

### anticipate-a-reel
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/anticipate-a-reel/
```ts
// @ts-nocheck
// Injected globals: ReelSetBuilder, SpeedPresets, CardSymbol, CARD_DECK,
//                   WILD_CARD, app, pickWeighted

const A = '7', B = '8', C = '9';
const S = 'scatter'; // scatter. registered as a custom card below
const IDS = [A, B, C, S];

const SCATTER_SYM = { id: S, color: 0xff6b35, label: 'SCAT', textColor: 0xffffff };

// Two scatters on reels 0 and 2. anticipation holds reels 3 and 4.
const GRID = [
  [S, A, B],
  [A, B, C],
  [B, S, A],
  [C, A, B],
  [B, C, A],
];

const reelSet = new ReelSetBuilder()
  .reels(5)
  .visibleRows(3)
  .symbolSize(90, 90)
  .symbolGap(4, 4)
  .symbols(r => {
    for (const sym of [...CARD_DECK, WILD_CARD, SCATTER_SYM]) {
      r.register(sym.id, CardSymbol, { color: sym.color, label: sym.label, textColor: sym.textColor });
    }
  })
  .speed('normal', SpeedPresets.NORMAL)
  .speed('turbo', SpeedPresets.TURBO)
  .ticker(app.ticker)
  .build();

// Longer anticipationDelay so the "hold" on reels 3+4 is clearly visible.
reelSet.speed.addProfile('demo', { ...SpeedPresets.NORMAL, anticipationDelay: 1800 });
reelSet.setSpeed('demo');

return {
  reelSet,
  onSpin: async () => {
    const p = reelSet.spin();
    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 220));
    reelSet.setAnticipation([3, 4]);
    reelSet.setResult(GRID.map((visible) => ({ visible })));
    await p;
  },
};
```

### anticipation-teaser
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/anticipation-teaser/
```ts
// @ts-nocheck
// Injected globals: ReelSetBuilder, SpeedPresets, CardSymbol, CARD_DECK,
//                   WILD_CARD, app
//
// Anticipation teaser. pair setAnticipation with a buffer-above prefill so
// a slow reel approaches a known high-value symbol. The user literally sees
// the bonus coming as the reel decelerates.

const COIN = 'coin';
const COIN_CARD = { id: COIN, color: 0xfacc15, label: 'BIG', textColor: 0x6b5400 };

const CARD_IDS = CARD_DECK.map((c) => c.id);
function rv() { return CARD_IDS[Math.floor(Math.random() * CARD_IDS.length)]; }

const reelSet = new ReelSetBuilder()
  .reels(5)
  .visibleRows(3)
  .symbolSize(80, 80)
  .symbolGap(4, 4)
  .bufferSymbols(1)
  .symbols((r) => {
    for (const sym of [...CARD_DECK, WILD_CARD, COIN_CARD]) {
      r.register(sym.id, CardSymbol, { color: sym.color, label: sym.label, textColor: sym.textColor });
    }
  })
  .speed('normal', SpeedPresets.NORMAL)
  .speed('turbo', SpeedPresets.TURBO)
  .ticker(app.ticker)
  .build();

// Long anticipation delay so the teaser cell is clearly visible above the
// decelerating reel. Same pattern as the base anticipate-a-reel recipe.
reelSet.speed.addProfile('demo', { ...SpeedPresets.NORMAL, anticipationDelay: 1800 });
reelSet.setSpeed('demo');

return {
  reelSet,
  onSpin: async () => {
    // Land result. reels 3 and 4 will anticipate.
    // Explicit ColumnTarget form: bufferAbove on reels 3 and 4 holds the BIG
    // coin, so when those reels decelerate for the anticipation phase it is
    // visible at the top edge, "approaching" the visible area.
    const result = [
      { visible: [rv(), rv(), rv()] },
      { visible: [rv(), rv(), rv()] },
      { visible: [rv(), rv(), rv()] },
      { visible: [rv(), rv(), rv()], bufferAbove: [COIN] },
      { visible: [rv(), rv(), rv()], bufferAbove: [COIN] },
    ];

    const p = reelSet.spin();
    await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 220));
    reelSet.setAnticipation([3, 4]);
    // `result` is already ColumnTarget[] (it carries bufferAbove on reels
    // 3 and 4). Do NOT re-wrap. previous code wrote
    // `setResult(result.map((visible) => ({ visible })))` which nested each
    // ColumnTarget inside another visible-only wrapper, silently discarding
    // bufferAbove and breaking the COIN teaser.
    reelSet.setResult(result);
    await p;
  },
};
```

### big-symbol-cascade-fall
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/big-symbol-cascade-fall/
```ts
// @ts-nocheck
// Injected globals: ReelSetBuilder, SpeedPresets, CardSymbol, CARD_DECK,
//                   PIXI, app

// CASCADE-FALL pattern.
//
// A tall 1x3 wild lands with its anchor in bufferAbove. tail visible
// at row 0. The other reels land a 3-of-a-kind cluster on a row BELOW
// the wild's tail. The cluster wins, the cells clear, and the cascade
// refill drops the wild downward into full visibility.
//
// This is the "big symbol falls when supporting cells are cleared"
// beat. common in cluster / tumble slots where high-value symbols
// reveal themselves over multiple cascade chains.
//
// What this proves:
//   - `runCascade`'s `nextGrid` callback can return a grid that
//     repositions a big-symbol anchor. moving it from bufferAbove[1]
//     (row -2) to visible[0] (row 0) in one cascade step.
//   - `_coordinateBigSymbols` runs on the refill grid the same as on
//     a setResult grid. buffer-row anchors are accepted, OCCUPIED
//     stubs painted across the moved block's new position.
//   - The visual block "falls" because the refill animation drops
//     each strip cell, including the anchor sprite, into its new slot.

const TALL = { id: 'tall', color: 0xff8c42, label: 'TALL', textColor: 0x4a1d00, w: 1, h: 3 };
const MATCH = { id: 'match', color: 0x4ade80, label: 'MATCH', textColor: 0x0a4a1d };
const REELS = 3;
const ROWS = 4;
const SIZE = 76;
const GAP = 4;

const reelSet = new ReelSetBuilder()
  .reels(REELS)
  .visibleRows(ROWS)
  // Anchor at bufferAbove[1] (row -2) needs bufferAbove >= 2.
  .bufferSymbols(2)
  .symbolSize(SIZE, SIZE)
  .symbolGap(GAP, GAP)
  .symbols((registry) => {
    for (const card of CARD_DECK) {
      registry.register(card.id, CardSymbol, {
        color: card.color, label: card.label, textColor: card.textColor,
      });
    }
    registry.register(TALL.id, CardSymbol, {
      color: TALL.color, label: TALL.label, textColor: TALL.textColor,
    });
    registry.register(MATCH.id, CardSymbol, {
      color: MATCH.color, label: MATCH.label, textColor: MATCH.textColor,
    });
  })
  .weights(Object.fromEntries(CARD_DECK.map((c, i) => [c.id, 12 - i])))
  .symbolData({ [TALL.id]: { weight: 0, zIndex: 5, size: { w: TALL.w, h: TALL.h } } })
  .speed('normal', { ...SpeedPresets.NORMAL, bounceDistance: 0, bounceDuration: 0 })
  .tumble({
    fall:   { duration: 320, ease: 'power3.in',  rowStagger: 60 },
    dropIn: { duration: 480, ease: 'power3.out', rowStagger: 60, distance: 'perHole' },
  })
  .ticker(app.ticker)
  .build();

const FILLER_IDS = ['7', '8', '9', '10', 'J'];
const filler = () => FILLER_IDS[Math.floor(Math.random() * FILLER_IDS.length)];

return {
  reelSet,
  onSpin: async () => {
    // ── 1. Initial spin: tall wild on reel 0 with tail at row 0; ─────
    //      plant a MATCH cluster across all 3 reels at row 1.
    const initialGrid = [
      // Reel 0: anchor at bufferAbove[1] = row -2. Block spans rows
      // -2, -1, 0. Tail at visible[0]. Plant MATCH at row 1; fillers
      // at rows 2, 3.
      {
        visible: [filler(), MATCH.id, filler(), filler()],
        bufferAbove: [undefined, TALL.id],
      },
      // Reel 1: MATCH at row 1.
      { visible: [filler(), MATCH.id, filler(), filler()] },
      // Reel 2: MATCH at row 1.
      { visible: [filler(), MATCH.id, filler(), filler()] },
    ];
    const spinDone = reelSet.spin();
    await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 240));
    reelSet.setResult(initialGrid);
    await spinDone;
    await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 900));

    // ── 2. Cascade: MATCH row clears, wild falls. ─────────────────────
    //
    // `runCascade` runs `detectWinners` → `destroySymbols` → `nextGrid`
    // → refill, repeating until detectWinners returns []. We script a
    // single round here: row 1 across all 3 reels is the winning
    // cluster, and nextGrid moves the wild block to visible[0..2].
    let chained = false;
    reelSet.setDropOrder('all');
    await reelSet.runCascade({
      detectWinners: () => {
        if (chained) return [];
        chained = true;
        return [0, 1, 2].map((reel) => ({ reel, row: 1 }));
      },
      nextGrid: () => [
        // Reel 0: block now at rows 0, 1, 2 (fully visible). New top
        // cell in bufferAbove[0]; the coordinator paints OCCUPIED at
        // visible[1] and [2] so 'tall' here is the anchor only.
        {
          visible: [TALL.id, filler(), filler(), filler()],
          bufferAbove: [filler()],
        },
        // Reels 1, 2: fresh random fillers.
        { visible: [filler(), filler(), filler(), filler()] },
        { visible: [filler(), filler(), filler(), filler()] },
      ],
      pauseAfterDestroyMs: 280,
    });
  },
};
```

### big-symbol-held-respin
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/big-symbol-held-respin/
```ts
// @ts-nocheck
// Injected globals: ReelSetBuilder, SpeedPresets, CardSymbol, CARD_DECK,
//                   PIXI, app

// NUDGE-IN, THEN HOLD a buffer-anchored big symbol across a respin.
//
// NUDGE-IN, THEN HOLD a buffer-anchored big symbol across a respin.
// Reel 3 lands with a tall wild whose anchor sits in bufferAbove (only
// the tail of the block shows at row 0). The player nudges to drag the
// wild into full view, then gets a re-spin of the OTHER reels while
// reel 3 is held. The held reel preserves the now-revealed block across
// the respin because `SpinOptions.holdReels` skips START/SPIN/STOP on
// the held column. the strip array, the anchor's size, and the
// occupancy map all carry through.
//
// Sequence:
//   1. Spin all reels; land tail-visible 1x3 wild on reel 2 (column index 2).
//   2. Player nudges reel 2 down by 2 to drag the block into full view.
//   3. Respin reels 0, 1, 3, 4 with `holdReels: [2]`. Reel 2 stays put
//      (now-revealed block intact, fully visible).
//
// What this proves:
//   - `setResult` accepts a big-symbol anchor in `bufferAbove`; the
//     coordinator paints OCCUPIED stubs across the strip.
//   - `nudge()` slides the block as a unit from its buffer-anchored
//     landing into fully-visible state (strip rotates by `distance`).
//   - `holdReels` + a big-symbol block: the held reel's strip array,
//     occupancy map, and resized anchor sprite all survive the no-op
//     spin. The block stays exactly where the nudge left it.
//   - `_coordinateBigSymbols` runs on the result grid passed by the
//     player for the non-held reels; the held column's placeholder entry
//     is ignored.

const TALL = { id: 'tall', color: 0xff8c42, label: 'TALL', textColor: 0x4a1d00, w: 1, h: 3 };
const REELS = 5;
const HELD_REEL = 2;
const ROWS = 3;
const SIZE = 80;
const GAP = 4;

const reelSet = new ReelSetBuilder()
  .reels(REELS)
  .visibleRows(ROWS)
  // bufferAbove >= 2 lets the 1x3 anchor sit at row -2 (block extends
  // through row 0. tail visible).
  .bufferSymbols(2)
  .symbolSize(SIZE, SIZE)
  .symbolGap(GAP, GAP)
  .symbols((registry) => {
    for (const card of CARD_DECK) {
      registry.register(card.id, CardSymbol, {
        color: card.color, label: card.label, textColor: card.textColor,
      });
    }
    registry.register(TALL.id, CardSymbol, {
      color: TALL.color, label: TALL.label, textColor: TALL.textColor,
    });
  })
  .weights(Object.fromEntries(CARD_DECK.map((c, i) => [c.id, 12 - i])))
  .symbolData({ [TALL.id]: { weight: 0, zIndex: 5, size: { w: TALL.w, h: TALL.h } } })
  .speed('normal', { ...SpeedPresets.NORMAL, bounceDistance: 0, bounceDuration: 0 })
  .ticker(app.ticker)
  .build();

const FILLER_IDS = ['7', '8', '9', '10', 'J'];
const filler = () => FILLER_IDS[Math.floor(Math.random() * FILLER_IDS.length)];
const ct = () => ({ visible: [filler(), filler(), filler()] });

return {
  reelSet,
  onSpin: async () => {
    // ── 1. Initial spin lands the tail-visible wild on reel 2. ──────
    const initialGrid = [
      ct(), ct(),
      // Reel 2: anchor at bufferAbove[1] = row -2. Block spans rows
      // -2, -1, 0. Only row 0 shows the block's bottom cell.
      { visible: [filler(), filler(), filler()], bufferAbove: [undefined, TALL.id] },
      ct(), ct(),
    ];
    const spin1 = reelSet.spin();
    await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 240));
    reelSet.setResult(initialGrid);
    await spin1;
    await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 900));

    // ── 2. Nudge the held reel DOWN by 2 to reveal the full block. ──
    //
    // Survival check (down): anchor strip index (0) + h - 1 + distance =
    // 0 + 3 - 1 + 2 = 4 < total 7 ✓. The block moves as a unit from
    // strip[0..2] to strip[2..4]. Visible[0..2] = ['tall', 'tall', 'tall'].
    await reelSet.nudge(HELD_REEL, {
      distance: 2,
      direction: 'down',
      incoming: [filler(), filler()],
      duration: 620,
    });

    // ── 3. Respin everything EXCEPT reel 2. ─────────────────────────
    //
    // `holdReels: [HELD_REEL]` tells the engine to skip START/SPIN/STOP
    // on reel 2 entirely. The grid we pass below MUST include all 5
    // columns (every shipped slot expects a full grid), but the entry at
    // index 2 is ignored. the held reel keeps whatever it had.
    //
    // The block on reel 2 (anchor at strip[0], stubs at strip[1..2])
    // survives because:
    //   - no motion runs on reel 2 (strip array unchanged)
    //   - `_finalizeFrame` doesn't re-run (occupancy unchanged)
    //   - the anchor's sized sprite stays at its post-land dimensions
    //
    // We hand the held column a placeholder ColumnTarget; the held
    // entry's contents are not validated against the current strip
    // and are simply dropped during frame building.
    const respinGrid = [
      ct(), ct(),
      ct(), // ignored. reel 2 is held
      ct(), ct(),
    ];
    const spin2 = reelSet.spin({ holdReels: [HELD_REEL] });
    await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 240));
    reelSet.setResult(respinGrid);
    await spin2;
    await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 900));
  },
};
```

### big-symbol-partial-land
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/big-symbol-partial-land/
```ts
// @ts-nocheck
// Injected globals: ReelSetBuilder, SpeedPresets, CardSymbol, CARD_DECK,
//                   PIXI, app

// PARTIAL-LAND pattern.
//
// A tall 1x3 wild lands with its ANCHOR in bufferAbove. most of the
// block is hidden above the visible window, only its bottom cell shows
// at row 0 ("tail visible"). The player nudges DOWN by 2 to drag the
// whole block into view, then nudges UP by 2 to push it back into
// hiding.
//
// This is enabled by:
//   - `_coordinateBigSymbols` scans the full strip range (including
//     bufferAbove and bufferBelow) for big-symbol anchors. The user
//     supplies the anchor at `bufferAbove[1]`; the engine paints
//     OCCUPIED at `bufferAbove[0]` and `visible[0]` automatically.
//   - `_finalizeFrame` sizes anchors that sit in bufferAbove with the
//     block's body extending into visible. The mask clips the off-screen
//     portion; the visible portion of the sprite shows through.
//   - `getVisibleSymbols` resolves visible row 0 to the anchor's id via
//     a NEGATIVE `anchorRow` in `_occupancy`.

const TALL = { id: 'tall', color: 0xff8c42, label: 'TALL', textColor: 0x4a1d00, w: 1, h: 3 };
const REELS = 5;
const ROWS = 3;
const SIZE = 80;
const GAP = 4;

const reelSet = new ReelSetBuilder()
  .reels(REELS)
  .visibleRows(ROWS)
  // Need bufferAbove >= 2 so the 1x3 block's anchor can sit at row -2
  // with the block extending through row 0.
  .bufferSymbols(2)
  .symbolSize(SIZE, SIZE)
  .symbolGap(GAP, GAP)
  .symbols((registry) => {
    for (const card of CARD_DECK) {
      registry.register(card.id, CardSymbol, {
        color: card.color, label: card.label, textColor: card.textColor,
      });
    }
    registry.register(TALL.id, CardSymbol, {
      color: TALL.color, label: TALL.label, textColor: TALL.textColor,
    });
  })
  .weights(Object.fromEntries(CARD_DECK.map((c, i) => [c.id, 12 - i])))
  .symbolData({ [TALL.id]: { weight: 0, zIndex: 5, size: { w: TALL.w, h: TALL.h } } })
  // Big symbols don't tolerate the default landing bounce. zero it.
  .speed('normal', { ...SpeedPresets.NORMAL, bounceDistance: 0, bounceDuration: 0 })
  .ticker(app.ticker)
  .build();

const FILLER_IDS = ['7', '8', '9', '10', 'J'];
const filler = () => FILLER_IDS[Math.floor(Math.random() * FILLER_IDS.length)];
const ct = () => ({ visible: [filler(), filler(), filler()] });

return {
  reelSet,
  onSpin: async () => {
    // 1. Land the 1x3 TALL with anchor at `bufferAbove[1]` (= row -2).
    //    Block spans rows -2, -1, 0. Only visible row 0 shows the block's
    //    bottom cell; rows 1 and 2 are random fillers.
    //
    //    The engine paints OCCUPIED at row -1 and row 0 automatically;
    //    we leave `bufferAbove[0]` undefined and `visible[0]` as filler
    //    (both get overwritten by the coordinator).
    //
    //    Every column is a ColumnTarget; reels that do not need buffer
    //    entries pass `{ visible: [...] }` alone.
    const grid = [
      ct(), ct(),
      { visible: [filler(), filler(), filler()], bufferAbove: [undefined, TALL.id] },
      ct(), ct(),
    ];
    const p = reelSet.spin();
    await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 220));
    reelSet.setResult(grid);
    await p;
    await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 700));

    // 2. Nudge DOWN by 2. anchor moves from row -2 to row 0; block now
    //    fills visible rows 0, 1, 2. Fully visible.
    //
    //    Survival check (down): anchor strip index + h - 1 + distance < total
    //    (0 + 3 - 1 + 2 = 4 < 7). total = bufferAbove(2) + visibleRows(3) +
    //    bufferBelow(2). The block stays on the strip end-to-end.
    //
    //    `incoming` is the new visible-area content arriving from the top.
    //    Buffer slots and big-symbol cells (anchor / OCCUPIED stubs) are
    //    protected during pre-placement, so any incoming entries that would
    //    land on a protected slot are dropped. Here every visible row is
    //    consumed by the block, so the incoming pair is consumed by the
    //    wrap pipeline (queue → random buffer fill) rather than appearing
    //    on screen. Pass real ids regardless; the engine ignores unused ones.
    await reelSet.nudge(2, {
      distance: 2,
      direction: 'down',
      incoming: [filler(), filler()],
      duration: 640,
    });
    await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 800));

    // 3. Nudge UP by 2. anchor moves back from row 0 to row -2.
    //    Survival check (up): anchor strip index - distance >= 0 (2 - 2 = 0).
    //    Block returns to tail-visible state.
    await reelSet.nudge(2, {
      distance: 2,
      direction: 'up',
      incoming: [filler(), filler()],
      duration: 540,
    });
  },
};
```

### big-symbols-mxn-giant
URL: https://pixi-reels.schmooky.dev/recipes/big-symbols-mxn-giant/
```ts
// @ts-nocheck
// Injected globals: ReelSetBuilder, SpeedPresets, CardSymbol, CARD_DECK,
//                   SharedRectMaskStrategy, PIXI, gsap, app, textures,
//                   blurTextures, SYMBOL_IDS, pickWeighted
//
// GIANT big symbol. 3×3. Mid-board feature anchor: jackpot reveal panels,
// "free spins triggered" overlays, large character art. Eats nine cells out
// of a 6×5 grid, so weight 0 and place sparingly via the server.

const GIANT = { id: 'giant', color: 0xfff3a0, label: 'GIANT', textColor: 0x6b5400, w: 3, h: 3 };
const REELS = 6;
const ROWS = 5;
const SIZE = 70;
const GAP = 3;

const reelSet = new ReelSetBuilder()
  .reels(REELS)
  .visibleRows(ROWS)
  .symbolSize(SIZE, SIZE)
  .symbolGap(GAP, GAP)
  .maskStrategy(new SharedRectMaskStrategy())
  .symbols((registry) => {
    for (const card of CARD_DECK) {
      registry.register(card.id, CardSymbol, { color: card.color, label: card.label });
    }
    registry.register(GIANT.id, CardSymbol, {
      color: GIANT.color, label: GIANT.label, textColor: GIANT.textColor,
    });
  })
  .weights(Object.fromEntries(CARD_DECK.map((c, i) => [c.id, 12 - i])))
  .symbolData({ [GIANT.id]: { weight: 0, zIndex: 5, size: { w: GIANT.w, h: GIANT.h } } })
  // Big symbols are visually massive (a 3x3 anchor is ~3x cell size);
  // the default 56px landing bounce overshoots into adjacent cells and
  // reads as a broken landing. Zero the bounce so the anchor lands
  // flush on grid.
```
